steps.description: Configure the OIDC client — the offline_access scope (set in .env) enables refresh tokens
Keep reactive state in sync with automatic silent renewal
Build the token-refresh UI using the useAuth composable
Load environment variables from .env (searches parent dirs so `dotnet run --project src` finds the .env at the sample root). Existing env vars win.
steps.code: Env.TraversePath().NoClobber().Load();

steps.description: Configure cookie auth + OpenID Connect against your SecureAuth workspace
Wire routes for sign-in redirect, user display, and sign-out
Declarative OIDC client registration. spring-dotenv resolves ${CLIENT_ID}, ${CLIENT_SECRET}, ${ISSUER_URL}, ${SCOPES} from the .env file at the sample root.
steps.code:
spring:
  security:
    oauth2:
      client:
        registration:
          secureauth:
            client-id: "{{CLIENT_ID}}"
            client-secret: "{{CLIENT_SECRET}}"
            scope: "{{SCOPES}}"
            authorization-grant-type: authorization_code
            redirect-uri: "{baseUrl}/login/oauth2/code/{registrationId}"
        provider:
          secureauth:
            issuer-uri: "{{ISSUER_URL}}"
            user-name-attribute: sub

steps.description: Spring Boot entry point — auto-configures the OAuth2 client, web server, and security filter chain.
SecurityFilterChain enables oauth2Login() for Authorization Code + PKCE and wires RP-initiated logout via OidcClientInitiatedLogoutSuccessHandler.
Home renders the signed-in page using claims from OidcUser. Spring Security already redirects unauthenticated requests to the authorization endpoint, so there's no signed-out branch here.
Discover the OIDC provider and configure the client
Build the authorization URL with PKCE and a CSRF state
Exchange the authorization code for tokens (PKCE + client_secret)
Wire the OIDC helpers into Express routes with a server-managed session
Import the ASP.NET Core OIDC/cookie middleware plus Duende.IdentityModel (used later for the refresh-token grant) and DotNetEnv for `.env` loading
Configure cookie auth + OpenID Connect; wire OnValidatePrincipal so every authenticated request can auto-refresh the access token
Swap a refresh token for a fresh access token via the OIDC token endpoint
Routes: user display, sign-in, manual refresh (POST /refresh), sign-out
Auto-refresh via the cookie auth OnValidatePrincipal event — runs on every authenticated request

Declarative OIDC client registration with offline_access scope so SecureAuth issues refresh tokens. spring-dotenv resolves ${CLIENT_ID}, ${CLIENT_SECRET}, ${ISSUER_URL}, ${SCOPES} from the .env file at the sample root.
steps.code:
spring:
  security:
    oauth2:
      client:
        registration:
          secureauth:
            client-id: "{{CLIENT_ID}}"
            client-secret: "{{CLIENT_SECRET}}"
            scope: "{{SCOPES}}"
            authorization-grant-type: authorization_code
            redirect-uri: "{baseUrl}/login/oauth2/code/{registrationId}"
        provider:
          secureauth:
            issuer-uri: "{{ISSUER_URL}}"
            user-name-attribute: sub

steps.description: Spring Boot entry point — same shape as the login-auth-code sample; imports Spring Security's OAuth2 client + authorized-client APIs used below for refresh.
SecurityFilterChain enables oauth2Login() for Authorization Code + PKCE and wires RP-initiated logout via OidcClientInitiatedLogoutSuccessHandler.

OAuth2AuthorizedClientManager enables Spring's background auto-refresh on any authorized-client use. A separate RefreshTokenOAuth2AuthorizedClientProvider bean is injected into RefreshController for on-demand refresh from POST /refresh.
Home renders the signed-in page, reading the access-token expiry from the stored OAuth2AuthorizedClient and surfacing a POST /refresh button.

POST /refresh loads the current OAuth2AuthorizedClient, builds an OAuth2AuthorizationContext, and invokes RefreshTokenOAuth2AuthorizedClientProvider.authorize(...) directly — forcing the refresh grant regardless of token expiry. On failure, renders an inline error page with the IdP's error code.
Discover the OIDC provider and configure the client
Build the authorization URL with PKCE and a CSRF state
Exchange the authorization code for tokens (access + refresh + id token + expiry)
Swap a refresh token for a fresh access token (and possibly a rotated refresh token)
Wire the OIDC helpers into Express routes with a server-managed session and POST /refresh
